# Philosophy & Technology — Author Guidelines (Synthetic)

## Scope

Philosophy & Technology publishes philosophical research on all aspects of technology, including AI, information technology, biotechnology, and their social and ethical implications. The journal bridges analytic and Continental traditions.

## Manuscript Requirements

- **Length:** 7,000–11,000 words including references.
- **Format:** Springer's standard format. APA-style citations preferred.
- **Abstract:** 150–250 words.
- **Keywords:** 4–6 keywords.
- **Structure:** Standard academic sections. Numbered or unnumbered headings acceptable.

## Review Process

- Double-blind peer review.
- Typical turnaround: 6–10 weeks.
- Desk rejection rate: approximately 25%.

## Preferred Topics (Recent Issues)

- Philosophy of artificial intelligence
- Digital ethics and governance
- Philosophy of information
- Phenomenology of digital technologies
- Responsible innovation frameworks
- Simondon, posthumanism, and technical mediation

## Special Features

- Topical collections on emerging themes (AI ethics, data governance, platform studies).
- Hybrid open access available.
- Active engagement with both empirical and purely philosophical contributions.

## Citation Ecology

Broad citation base expected: philosophy of technology (Ihde, Verbeek, Floridi, Stiegler), STS, AI/ML literature, and information ethics. Cross-referencing recent Philosophy & Technology publications is valued.
